author: Cox, JA; Jones, CD
title: Home range and survival characteristics of male Bachman's Sparrows in an old-growth forest managed with breeding season burns
Study_Characteristics:
Compared median size of home ranges between home ranges located in burned or unburned areas. "Territories where >75% of the ground
cover was eliminated were classified as burned,
whereas territories where <25% of the ground cover was eliminated were classified as unburned." pg. 265
cover was eliminated were classified as unburned.
Response_description: Mean home range size,  quantified using 95% fixed kernel. GPS locations used in home range analysis determined by searching for banded individuals.
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided
Maximum_treatment_burn_age: 4 months (First burns in May, last tracking date each year in August)
Comparator_fire_history:
Unburnt in the year of data collection. "Approximately half the tract
(40 ha) is burned each year in an alternating
manner (half not burned)." pg 264
Comments: Home range given in hectares. Authors note some skewness in size, and report medians as well as means.

author: Kelt, DA; Kelly, PA; Phillips, SE; Williams, DF
title: Home range size and habitat selection of reintroduced Sylvilagus bachmani riparius
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range size 90 days pre and  days post fire. Captive born rabbits released into refuge property. Refuge property was previously cultivated, has since been revegetated and replanted. Captive born rabbits were progeny of wild caught rabbits.
Response_description: Mean home range size, estimated using fixed kernel methods.
Tracking_method: VHF. Position estimation via handheld and triangulation
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided
Comparator_fire_history: Unburnt by this specific fire event. No other details provided.
Comments: Authors provide total number of fixes used in home range analysis across all seasons and all individuals, but don't specify for the 90 days pre and post fire.

author: Flaherty, JP; Gienger, CM
title: HABITAT SELECTION AND MOVEMENT PATTERNS OF COPPERHEADS (AGKISTRODON CONTORTRIX) IN FIRE-ALTERED LANDSCAPES
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewer. Coordinates taken from google pin of LBL Recreational Area
Study_Characteristics:
Compared home range size of males snakes in areas burnt by two prescribed fires and areas not burned during these fires. Snakes relocated between mid May and early October. "We considered snakes in either
Burn or Control when they spent at least 65% of their
time in one of the habitat types." pg. 151
Response_description: Mean home range size, estimated using 95% fixed kernel density
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not quantified, authors suggest fires were low intensity. Ignited by dropped incendiaries from helicopter.
Maximum_treatment_burn_age: ~8.5 years (First burn in April 2007, last field season October 2015)
Comparator_fire_history: Unburnt by these fire events. No other details provided.
Data_source: Means, sample size, SEM and mean tracking period calculated from data in Table 3. Pg 155.
Comments: Means, sample size, SEM and mean tracking period calculated from data in Table 3. Pg 155.

author: Flaherty, JP; Gienger, CM
title: HABITAT SELECTION AND MOVEMENT PATTERNS OF COPPERHEADS (AGKISTRODON CONTORTRIX) IN FIRE-ALTERED LANDSCAPES
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewer. Coordinates taken from google pin of LBL Recreational Area
Study_Characteristics:
Compared  mean daily distance moved of males snakes in areas burnt by two prescribed fires and areas not burned during these fires. Snakes relocated between mid May and early October. "We considered snakes in either
Burn or Control when they spent at least 65% of their
time in one of the habitat types." pg. 152
Response_description:
Mean daily distance moved. Calculates as "as the
distance between relocations divided by the number
of days elapsed between relocations" pg. 152
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not quantified, authors suggest fires were low intensity. Ignited by dropped incendiaries from helicopter.
Maximum_treatment_burn_age: ~8.5 years (First burn in April 2007, last field season October 2015)
Comparator_fire_history: Unburnt by these fire events. No other details provided.
Data_source: Means, sample size, SEM and mean tracking period calculated from data in Table 3. Pg 155.
Comments: Means, sample size, SEM and mean tracking period calculated from data in Table 3. Pg 155. See calculation sheet.

author: Körtner, G; Pavey, CR; Geiser, F
title: Spatial ecology of the mulgara in arid Australia:: impact of fire history on home range size and burrow use
Study_Characteristics: Estimated home range size for mulgaras occurring in mature and burned habitat. Authors do not compare home range sizes in control and burned habitat, but provided % of records in burnt areas and % of MCP burnt (Table 2, pg. 354). Following other studies, have classified individuals with >65% of MCP burned as burnt and <65% of MCP burned as control.
Response_description: Mean home range size, 90% MCP
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided
Maximum_treatment_burn_age: 3 years, 10 months (Burn in October 2002, last tracking date 21/08/2006)
Comments: Means, sample sizes and SEM calculated from Table 3, pg 354, excluding individual M6 due to lack of data. See calculation sheet. Mean tracking period calculated from Table 1, pg. 353 using number of days monitored, excluding individual M6 sue to lack of data.

author: Jansen, R; Little, RM; Crowe, TM
title: Habitat utilization and home range of the redwing francolin, Francolinus levaillantii, in highland grasslands, Mpumalanga province, South Africa
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewer. Coordinates estimated from map provided at Figure 1.
Study_Characteristics: Estimated home range size on two grassland farms, one which is burned tri-annually and the other burned annually and high intensity grazing.
Response_description: Mean home range size 95% kernel analysis
Impact_fire_characteristics: Annual prescribed burned
Comparator_fire_history: Grassland burned triannually, date of last burn not provided.
Comments: SEM caculated by reviwers from Table 1, pg. 332. Tracking data from Table 2. All birds tracked for 10 days. Impact site confounded with intense grazing. Both sites are grassland sites, although there is difference in land use, protected area (control) vs commerical grazing (impact).

else of either RE or ESAR had burned in the previous
20 years" pg. 27

author: Hope, B
title: Short-term response of the long-nosed bandicoot, Perameles nasuta, and the southern brown bandicoot, Isoodon obesulus obesulus, to low-intensity prescribed fire in heathland vegetation
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range size of two species of bandicoots before and after a low intensity prescribed fire.
Response_description: Mean home range size, 95% fixed kernel density
Impact_fire_characteristics: "Low intensity (typical flame height 0.3-2m)" pg. 732. Localised flare ups sorching canopy upto 15 m. Patchy, imcomplete combustion of leaf litter ~30% of burn area.
Comparator_fire_history: 18 years since last wildfire. No European prescribed burning has occurred in study area.
Comments: Mean home range, SEM mean relocations and minimum relocations calculated by reviwer from data in Table 3. Includes home range data from 2 individuals prefire with low number of relocations (authors note data insufficent) and 1 individual post fire also noted as insufficent data.

author: Kreling, SES; Gaynor, KM; McInturff, A; Calhoun, KL; Brashares, JS
title: Site fidelity and behavioral plasticity regulate an ungulate's response to extreme disturbance
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range size of black tailed deer in a natural experiment from the Mendocino Fire Complex
Response_description: Mean home range size, 95% local convex hull
Impact_fire_characteristics: Mendocino Fire Complex described as a megafire, at the date of publication "the second largest fire in California history" burning 1858 km2  pg. 15685.
Comparator_fire_history: Unburnt in the year of data collection. No other information provided.
Comments: Data was subset so each individual had same number of fixes before and after fire. Tracking periods before and after ranged from 15 to 25 days.

author: Kreling, SES; Gaynor, KM; McInturff, A; Calhoun, KL; Brashares, JS
title: Site fidelity and behavioral plasticity regulate an ungulate's response to extreme disturbance
Study_Characteristics: Compared short term displacement of deer within and outside the burned area before and during/three days after the passage of fire
Response_description:
Mean displacement during fire event and 3 day period post fire, calculated as follows " identified the point during the fire and the 3-day post-
fire period that was farthest from the pre-fire LoCoH home range
centroid and calculated the Euclidean distance between points" pg. 15687
Impact_fire_characteristics: Mendocino Fire Complex described as a megafire, at the date of publication "the second largest fire in California history" burning 1858 km2  pg. 15685.
Comparator_fire_history: Unburnt in the year of data collection. No other information provided.
Comments: Centriod displacement during the passage of fire and three day period post fire. Data was subset so each individual had same number of fixes before and after fire. Tracking periods before and after ranged from 15 to 25 days.

author: Roe, JH; Kish, AL; Nacy, JP
title: Variation and repeatability of home range in a forest-dwelling terrestrial turtle: implications for prescribed fire in forest management
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewers as half way point (straight line distance) between the two sites.
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range size of turtles at Lumber River State Park (unburned) and Weymouth Woods (burned)
Response_description: Home range size 95% kernel density
Impact_fire_characteristics: Prescribed fire used since 1974 as management tool. Typically low intensity, mean size 5.2 ha (+- SE 3.2 ha), and mean burn frequency 5.9 years between 2000 to 2016
Comparator_fire_history: No European application of fire since 2001.
Comments: Control and impact sites are aproximately 90km apart (euclidean). Impact site (Weymouth Woods) described as xeric uplands  "forest mosaic of mixed pine and hardwood forests" (pg. 72). Lumber River (control) "riverine bottomland swamp forest" with upland mixed pine and hardwood forests (pg. 72). Mean fixes calculated by reviewers using mean locations per year values provided in Home Range section of results (para 1, pg. 74).

author: Everitts, JL; Benson, TJ; Bednarz, JC; Anich, NM
title: Effects of Prescribed Burning on Swainson's Warbler Home-Range Size and Habitat Use
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewers, coordinates taken from the existing google pin of St Francis National Forest
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range size of warbler's with extensively burned home ranges (>50% burned) to those with unburned home ranges
Response_description: Home range size 95% fixed kernel
Impact_fire_characteristics: Low intensity, ignited with drip torches
Maximum_treatment_burn_age: 1.5 years (earliest burn in Winter 2007, last tracking period in July 2009)
Comparator_fire_history: No prescribed burning since 2004. No other information provided.
Comments: Two prescribed burns occurred during the study period, the first in winter 2007 and the second in winter 2009. Impact fire size calculated by reviewers as sum of both fire sizes. Mean fixes calculated by reviewer by averaging the mean number of fixes for 2008 and 2009.

author: Vernes, K; Pope, LC
title: Stability of nest range, home range and movement of the northern bettong (Bettongia tropica) following moderate-intensity fire in a tropical woodland, north-eastern Queensland
Study_Characteristics: Compared nest area sizes of bettongs before and after a prescribed fire.
Response_description: Nest area, 100% Minimum Convex Polygon
Impact_fire_characteristics: Low/moderate intensity, experimental
Comments: Mean nest areas calculated by reviewers from data in Table 2 pg. 144 using period 1 and period 3, excluding animals with insufficent number of fixes. In the before means, animals whose nest areas were not affected by fire were included. They were excluded in the after calculations. 0.0 values were excluded for both before and after mean calculations. Tracking data calculated by reviwers from fixes and months provided in Table 2, pg. 144. Nest areas included in mean area calculations were determined by Incremental Area Analysis by authors, and only those that approached asymptote considered as sufficent data by authors.

author: Hurteau, S; Sisk, T; Dickson, BG; Block, W
title: Variability in Nest Density, Occupancy, and Home Range Size of Western Bluebirds after Forest Treatments
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewer using google earth as the mid point between the 3 site locations given by the authors.
Study_Characteristics: Estimated home range size of western bluebirds in four treatments (burn, control, thin, and thin and burn)
Response_description: Home range size 90% fixed kernel
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided
Comments: Sample size assumed as 7 from total sample size of 28 across the four treatment groups. In all other plots, where the sample size is not equal, sample sizes are provided. Means and SE extracted from Figure 4 using Webplot digitiser. SEM calculated by subtracting the mean value extraxted from the plot from the SE +1 value extracted from the plot.

title: Response of Northern Bobwhite Movements to Management-Driven Disturbance in a Shrub-Dominated Ecosystem
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewer, coordinates taken as the approximate mid point of the Packsaddle Wildlife Management Area
Study_Characteristics: Estimated home range size of bobwhite coveys at various time since fire, used >36 months post fire as control group and 0-12 months as impact group
Response_description: Mean home range 95% Kernel density
Impact_fire_characteristics: Planned burns occurred at same time in 2013 and 2014. Mean size 254.3 ha (+/- 33.7 ha) of pastures burnt during 2013 and 2014

Comments: Fire impact size calculated as sum of 2013 and 2014 burns (pg. 176). author: Delay, SJ; Urquhart, O; Litzgus, JD
title: Wind farm and wildfire: spatial ecology of an endangered freshwater turtle in a recovering landscape
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewers. Authors withheld details of the site due to risk of illegal collection, however describe site as near the northern limit of the species distribution in Ontario. Coordinates taken from a non built up area near the St Lawerence River that is near the limit of the species northern distribution in Ontario
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range sizes of spotted turtles at two windfarm sites, one impacted by a wildfire during construction (2017-2019)
Response_description: Home range size 100% minimum convex polygon
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided
Data_source: Home range section of Results, pg. 129
Comments: Animals not tracked in winter at control site due to inaccessability. Mean fixes calculated by averaging the mean number of relocations per individual (Table A1), this does include individuals from a treatment third site (undistributed site) that data has not been extracted from. Exact date of fire not given, but occured sometime during windfarm construction (2017-2019), and maximum treatment age assumes fire was in 2017.

Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided

author: Jaffe, KE; Isbell, LA
title: After the Fire: Benefits of Reduced Ground Cover for Vervet Monkeys (Cercopithecus aethiops)
Study_Characteristics: Compared distance vervet monkeys travelled away from river towards burned or unburned habitat
Response_description: Maximum daily distance (m)
Impact_fire_characteristics: Fire largely undescribed by authors. Prefire grass height 20-93cm and post fire 0-5cm. Pre and post fire photos (Fig 1, pg 255) of the habitat indicate most of the grass layer was consumed, as well as consumption and sorching of shrublayer
Data_source: Ranging Behaviour and Rate of Movement section of results, paragraph 2 pg. 255
Comments: Sample sizes are the number of days, not individual monkeys.

author: Jaffe, KE; Isbell, LA
title: After the Fire: Benefits of Reduced Ground Cover for Vervet Monkeys (Cercopithecus aethiops)

Response_description: Distance travelled/30 mins
Impact_fire_characteristics: Fire largely undescribed by authors. Prefire grass height 20-93cm and post fire 0-5cm. Pre and post fire photos (Fig 1, pg 255) of the habitat indicate most of the grass layer was consumed, as well as consumption and sorching of shrublayer
Data_source: Ranging Behaviour and Rate of Movement section of results, last paragraph pg. 256
Comments: Sample sizes are taken from sampling of focal individuals.

author: Jaffe, KE; Isbell, LA
title: After the Fire: Benefits of Reduced Ground Cover for Vervet Monkeys (Cercopithecus aethiops)
Study_Characteristics: Compared maximum daily distance moved away from river by vervet monkeys before and after a wildfire
Response_description: Maximum daily distance (m)
Impact_fire_characteristics: Fire largely undescribed by authors. Prefire grass height 20-93cm and post fire 0-5cm. Pre and post fire photos (Fig 1, pg 255) of the habitat indicate most of the grass layer was consumed, as well as consumption and sorching of shrublayer
Data_source: Ranging Behaviour and Rate of Movement section of results, paragraph 2 pg. 255
Comments: Sample sizes are the number of days, not individual monkeys. Mean values and SEMs are the same as reported for CI, but ranges are different - unsure these are true values, or a copy/paste mistake.

author: Harris, KA; Clark, JD; Elmore, RD; Harper, CA
title: Direct and Indirect Effects of Fire on Eastern Box Turtles
Study_Characteristics: Compared home range size of eastern box turtles impacted by fire and those not impacted by fire at three sites in Tennessee
Response_description: Home range size 95% kernel density
Impact_fire_characteristics: 17 planned burns, one wildfire. Varing intensity (indicated by pyrometer tile temperature 62C to >184C)
Impact_fire_size: Wildfire 600 ha. Planned burn size not provided
Comparator_fire_history: History of prescribed fire (2-8 year fire return interval) across the three sites
Data_source: Means and SEM Table 3, pg. 1390. Sample size from Spatial Ecology section of results, para 2, pg. 1390
Comments: Animals tracked throughout the year, however tracking frequenct reduced to ~1 location/month December - April. Treatment and control groups spread across three sites which " varied in predominant vegetation types, topography, management, burn history, and burn regimes." pg. 1385

Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewers, coordinates taken from approximate mid point of Napa Valley

Response_measured: Distance between centroid and fire scar
Response_description: Distance between centroid and fire scar
Impact_fire_characteristics: Not provided
Comments: Before and after groups include owls with home ranges unimpacted and impacted by fire. Authors also analysed data of just owls with home ranges impacted by fire (too few repliactes for inclusion) and found little difference B: 1180 m (+-121 m) A: 1051m (+-211 m)

Comments: Calculated mean total distance moved, SEM, and tracking period data from Table 1, pg. 294. Used Burn and Control treatments for C and I. Burn treatment site received two prescribed burns before treatment burn, one 9 years pre treatment and another 6 years pre treatment.

author: Lovich, JE; Agha, M; Ennen, JR; Arundel, TR; Austin, M
title: Agassiz's desert tortoise (Gopherus agassizii) activity areas are little changed after wind turbine-induced fires in California
Lat_Long_Method: Estimated by reviewers. Coordinates taken from approximate mid point of Mesa Wind Project Site.
Study_Characteristics: Compared annual activity areas of tortoises before and after a wildfire caused by wind turbines
Response_description: Annual Activity Area, minimum convex polygon
Impact_fire_characteristics: 2 fires, both Ignited accidentally by wind turbines, one in summer another in autumn. Summer fire larger (152 ha vs 6.5 ha)
Comparator_fire_history: History of fire since 1992, creating vegetation mosaic. Various ignition causes (no details provided)
Comments: Tracking effort varied seasonally, with increased effort in nesting season Spring/Summer
